edges.description: Section A describes the RPV as containing the SGs, while Section B elaborates on the RPV's internal components, including the SGs, establishing that the SG design parameters are a subset of the overall RPV design parameters.

Section A describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as containing two steam generators (SGs), while Section B specifies that the RPV houses the SGs along with other components, directly connecting the design parameters of both the RPV and SGs as co-located and functionally integrated within the same module.

Section A provides the design context and physical description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B discusses the operational procedures developed to ensure that the RPV's pressure-temperature limits—derived from its design parameters—are not exceeded, directly connecting the design values from Section A to the regulatory compliance actions in Section B.

Section A and Section B both describe components housed within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), specifically noting that the RPV contains two steam generators (SGs), thus establishing that the SG design parameters (Table 5.4-1) are directly related to and must be compatible with the RPV design parameters (Table 5.3-1).

Section A describes the design parameters and components of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators and reactor pressure vessel, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure that the reactor vessel's pressure-temperature limits—derived from its design parameters—are not exceeded, thus connecting the design values from Section A to the operational requirements in Section B.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the steam generators (SGs) within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B outlines the requirement that operating procedures must ensure RPV pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, thus connecting the SG design parameters (A) as factors influencing the RPV's operational limits (B).

Section A discusses the system evaluation and optimization of steam generator (SG) parameters, while Section B describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as the component housing the SGs, establishing a structural relationship where the SG design parameters specified in Table 5.4-1 are physically contained within and must be compatible with the RPV design parameters in Table 5.3-1.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for the steam generators (SGs) to optimize plant performance, while Section B describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its integration with the SGs, indicating that the SG and RPV design parameters are interrelated components within the overall reactor coolant system.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.4 for further description of the steam generators, establishing a structural connection between the system evaluation and the detailed component design.

Section A describes the operational procedures and system components involving the steam generators (SGs), while Section B provides the structural and functional context of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), with both sections referencing the integration of SGs and RPV within the NuScale Power Module, thus connecting their design parameters as components of the same system.

Section A describes operational procedures involving the steam generators (SGs), while Section B outlines the design configuration of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its inclusion of SGs, connecting them through the shared component (SGs) and their respective design and operational parameters.

Both Section A and Section B reference the steam generators (SGs) and reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as key components of the NuScale Power Module, establishing their physical and functional integration, which is necessary context for comparing their respective design parameters as requested in the analysis question.

Section A describes the pressurizer as being integrated into the RPV upper shell and head, while Section B explains that the RPV contains both the steam generators (SGs) and the pressurizer, connecting the design and functional integration of these components.

Section A provides detailed material specifications for components that are generally described as part of the RPV and its subsystems in Section B, specifically linking the pressurizer and its integration with the RPV as outlined in the broader system context.

Section B describes the overall configuration of the RPV, explicitly stating that it contains two steam generators (SGs), thereby establishing that the SGs are integral components within the RPV whose design parameters (from Table 5.4-1 and Table 5.3-1) are directly related as part of the same system.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for the SG (Steam Generator) as a function of reactor power, while Section B describes operational steps involving the RPV (Reactor Pressure Vessel), indicating that both sections provide specific design and operational parameters for their respective components, which are to be compared as per the analysis question.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for the SG (steam generator) to optimize plant performance, while Section B details the design and material specifications of the RPV (reactor pressure vessel) and its components, allowing for a direct comparison of their respective design parameters as requested in the analysis question.

Section A discusses operational procedures involving the steam generators (SGs) and reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B provides material specifications for the RPV and its components, connecting them through their shared involvement in the RPV's design and operation.
